one_time_description: Contingent asset - specific claims tribunal settlement estimated at $1,400,000 (undiscounted) for wrongful severance of reserve interests by Federal Government
economic_notes: First Nation has investments in Mamalilikulla Economic Development Corporation and Mamalilikulla Economic Development LP (both wholly owned), plus partnerships in Nanwakolas Carbon Credit LP (23%), Nanwakolas Offset LP (26%), Nanwakolas Timber LP (21%), Nanwakolas Business Corporation (17%), Gwabalis Fisheries Society (25%). Nanwakolas Forestry LP was dissolved during the year. Significant logging transactions on Village Island TSL. Endowment fund balance $1,035,165 managed by Coast Funds. COVID-19 caused office closures, construction delays, and increased pandemic funding.
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2022 (not 2024). Prior year restated for error correction: BC Parks accounts receivable was overstated by $180,000 in 2021. Social Housing consolidated as subsidiary; 8 entities accounted for under modified equity method (GBEs/partnerships). Long-term debt of $78,331 is interest-free Government of Canada loan repayable upon specific claims settlement. Contingent asset of ~$1,400,000 from specific claims tribunal settlement agreement reached. No remuneration schedule found in provided text. Advances from related parties (Mamalilikulla Forestry Corporation and LP) total $2,085,597. Budget figures incomplete as Social Housing not budgeted.
